description: Operator-facing contract for the pressure-control evidence lane. It records what the runtime pressure snapshot, opt-in admission policy, region memory-budget pressure rows, RCH proof-lane pressure rows, no-local-RCH fallback evidence, scheduler pressure flamegraph attribution, deterministic lab scenario evidence, operator diagnostics bundle, and pressure triage runbook prove, what they deliberately do not prove, and which RCH-routed command verifies the artifact/docs/source alignment.

proof_lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_runtime_pressure_control_evidence_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test runtime_pressure_control_evidence_contract -- --nocapture

scheduler_pressure_flamegraph_attributio.direct_main_trigger: Direct-main changes under src/runtime/scheduler/ or scheduler-pressure changes that cite scheduler_tail_pressure must attach the Phase 6 flamegraph artifact when the flamegraph gate is triggered.
supports: The pressure-control lane can point operators from scheduler_tail_pressure evidence to the exact direct-main flamegraph artifact required by the Phase 6 gate.
The attribution names the benchmark surface and scheduler-adjacent baseline rows that the artifact is allowed to support.
does_not_support: The flamegraph attribution does not prove a performance improvement, real-host throughput, absence of scheduler regressions, or autonomous scheduler rewrites.
The attribution is not required for docs-only pressure-control changes that do not touch a Phase 6 hot-path trigger.

no_local_rch_fallback_evidence.closeout_rule: A pressure-control proof lane may cite a green RCH Cargo proof only when the command starts with the remote-required prefix, uses an isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR, and the saved transcript or admission receipt rules out local Cargo fallback.
does_not_prove: No-local-RCH fallback evidence does not prove the RCH fleet is generally healthy or available.
No-local-RCH fallback evidence does not prove real-host throughput, production admission control, or scheduler performance.

operator_diagnostics_bundle.purpose: Give operators one compact pressure-control handoff that summarizes snapshot verdicts, admission decisions, region memory-budget pressure, RCH proof-lane pressure, deterministic replay evidence, validation fallback status, and scope limits without requiring scattered artifact inspection.

status_semantics.meaning: Live pressure evidence can guide triage, but does not by itself prove throughput, scheduler regression closure, or production-safe policy rollout.
The cited pressure diagnosis is paired with deterministic lab or replay evidence for the scenario family under discussion.
A deadlock claim is allowed only when explicit trapped-cycle proof is present; spectral fragmentation alone is insufficient.
A remote-required validation lane lacked workers or refused local fallback, so the operator report must name the first blocker instead of claiming a green proof.

operator_diagnostics_bundle.closeout_rule: Pressure-control closeouts should summarize the diagnostics bundle status, cite the strongest evidence class actually present, and preserve every non-claim before recommending any policy or scheduler follow-up.
does_not_prove: The diagnostics bundle does not prove real-host throughput or scheduler performance.
The diagnostics bundle does not prove production-on-by-default admission control, autonomous scheduler rewrites, or per-region allocator enforcement.
The diagnostics bundle does not prove a deadlock unless explicit trapped-cycle proof is cited.

proves: RuntimePressureSnapshot exposes a stable operator-facing schema version and deterministic aggregate classification fields.
Resource, scheduler, spectral, and platform probe signals are folded through the documented snapshot status model.
does_not_prove: A pressure snapshot alone does not prove real-host throughput, optimal scheduling, deadlock, or safe autonomous admission control.

proves: RuntimePressureLabScenarioEvidence covers deterministic classification behavior for the required scenario families under fixed seeds.
Stable JSON projection and fixed-input reproducibility are checked by unit tests in the runtime resource monitor module.
does_not_prove: Lab scenarios do not prove live host capacity, external service performance, or scheduler behavior outside their synthetic inputs.

proves: RuntimePressureAdmissionPolicy is disabled by default and emits deterministic RuntimePressureAdmissionDecision rows when explicitly enabled.
Required work remains admitted so cleanup, cancellation, finalizers, and region quiescence are not blocked by optional admission control.
Optional work fails closed for unknown pressure snapshot schemas.
does_not_prove: The opt-in admission policy does not prove production throughput, autonomous scheduler rewrites, or production-on-by-default admission control.

proves: Fragmented or critical spectral topology can require trapped-cycle investigation before escalation claims are made.
does_not_prove: Spectral fragmentation without explicit trapped-cycle proof is not a deadlock proof.
requires_additional_proof_for: operator escalations that cite proven trapped cycles

proves: RuntimePressureRchProofLaneSnapshot folds externally captured RCH worker admission receipts into redacted deterministic pressure rows.
Remote-required proof-lane refusals preserve local-fallback refusal as critical advisory pressure without probing RCH from the core runtime.
does_not_prove: RCH proof-lane pressure rows do not prove real-host throughput, worker fleet availability, production admission control, or automatic proof-lane scheduling.

proves: Pressure-control proof lanes name the remote-required command prefix and transcript markers needed before citing a green RCH Cargo proof.
Remote-required proof-lane refusals preserve receipt fields that make local Cargo fallback explicit and fail closed.
does_not_prove: No-local-RCH fallback evidence does not prove RCH fleet availability, real-host throughput, production admission control, or scheduler performance.

proves: The pressure triage runbook provides one compact operator diagnostics bundle that ties snapshot verdicts, admission decisions, memory-budget pressure, RCH proof-lane pressure, replay evidence, validation fallback status, and scope limits together.
The bundle names the evidence class operators may cite before making policy or scheduler recommendations.
does_not_prove: The diagnostics bundle does not prove real-host throughput, scheduler performance, production-on-by-default admission control, autonomous scheduler rewrites, per-region allocator enforcement, or deadlock without trapped-cycle proof.

proves: RuntimePressureRegionMemoryBudgetSnapshot folds explicitly declared region memory envelopes into deterministic advisory pressure rows.
Region memory-budget overrun evidence can make optional work rejectable through the opt-in admission policy while required cleanup, cancellation, finalizers, and quiescence work remain admitted.
does_not_prove: Region memory-budget pressure rows do not prove per-region allocator accounting, automatic allocation throttling, production throughput, or production-on-by-default admission control.

proves: Scheduler tail-pressure evidence is tied to the Phase 6 flamegraph gate by an explicit artifact path pattern, benchmark surface, and scheduler-adjacent baseline rows.
Operators can distinguish advisory scheduler pressure attribution from performance-improvement or throughput claims.
does_not_prove: Scheduler pressure flamegraph attribution does not prove real-host throughput, absence of scheduler regressions, a performance improvement, autonomous scheduler rewrites, or deadlock absence.
